description_long: This is a traditional Japanese samurai helmet, known as a kabuto, featuring a prominent pair of upward-curving, horn-like crests (maedate) at the front. The helmet bowl is ribbed and made of dark metal, with a central circular ornament on the forehead. The lower section is adorned with a series of overlapping, lacquered plates in a reddish-brown hue, providing neck and side protection. Decorative rivets and patterned cutouts embellish the neck guard, adding both structural integrity and visual interest. The helmet's construction suggests a combination of metal and lacquered leather, with intricate craftsmanship typical of historical samurai armor. This object would stand out in an urban context as a striking, historical artifact.
description: A traditional Japanese samurai kabuto helmet with horn-like crests, ribbed dark metal bowl, and a reddish-brown lacquered neck guard.
description_view_0: Front view shows the prominent horn-like crests, central circular ornament, and ribbed dome.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the layered neck guard plates and the side profile of the crests.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the full expanse of the overlapping neck guard plates and the ribbed dome.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the side profile of the crests and the detailed neck guard.
attribute_graphic_design: The helmet features a traditional Japanese motif with ribbed metal, horn-like crests, and patterned cutouts on the neck guard, but no modern graphics or text.
